behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_PROTECTR::look' (examine) handler for the entity: when the player examines the entity it emits a single bark line "Protector " and then the U8P_PROTECTR::look process ends (appears to suspend).
There are no dialogue branches, menus, or conditional logic in the U8P_PROTECTR::look handler.

The file also contains item_properties metadata describing a special weapon (baseDamage 5, damageModifier 13, types: blade and magic) that is present in the file but is not referenced by the U8P_PROTECTR::look() process.
notable_control_flow: U8P_PROTECTR::look is a process (processType 0x0208) that executes on examine/U8P_PROTECTR::look trigger.
Control flow is linear: emit a Bark ('Protector ') then invoke suspend/terminate of the process (suspend inferred from unknown node).
No flag reads/writes, no calls to other game systems, and no branching are present in the U8P_PROTECTR::look handler.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "Protector " intentional (e.g., prefix for concatenation) or an artifact of extraction?
What is the in-file item_properties weapon used for and why is it present in this script despite not being referenced by U8P_PROTECTR::look?

Does the engine always suspend the U8P_PROTECTR::look process immediately after barking here, or could the 'suspend' node represent an engine-yield that may allow other reactions (confidence: medium)?

main_function.name: U8P_PROTECTR::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the examine/lookup response for the entity: present a short descriptive bark to the player and end the U8P_PROTECTR::look interaction.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the game's U8P_PROTECTR::look/examine event on this NPC/object (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_PROTECTR::look
function_map.role: Handles the player's U8P_PROTECTR::look/examine action; emits a single bark and ends the process.
